_meta.licensing: Each entry's per-source license is in its `license` field and governs the archived raw bytes plus any verbatim source text quoted in `normalized_content` (e.g. `narrative_excerpt`, `declaration_text`). Those third-party excerpts remain under the source's own license (WHO CC BY-NC-SA 3.0 IGO; Imperial restricted-use assumption pending primary license footer; Wikipedia CC BY-SA 4.0; US CDC public domain). The CC BY 4.0 license declared for this repo in LICENSES.md covers only the schema, structure, SHA-256 hashes, and my own annotations in this file, not quoted third-party text or extracted third-party tables. Confirm permissions before commercial reuse or redistribution of restricted publisher material.
_meta.scope: Live provenance registry for public sources for the BDBV 2026 outbreak. Entries with raw_archive_status=public_bytes ship one fetched-and-archived byte payload under data/bundibugyo-2026/raw/<sha256>. Entries with raw_archive_status=private_restricted_bytes retain URL, timestamp, hash, and extracted factual metadata but do not redistribute publisher bytes.
_meta.verification_workflow: For public_bytes entries, re-run shasum -a 256 against the raw file to verify. For private_restricted_bytes entries, the content_hash records the original retrieval hash but the raw payload is intentionally not redistributed.

normalized_content.declaration_text: On 15 May 2026, the Ministry of Public Health, Hygiene and Social Welfare, DRC, officially declared
normalized_content.narrative_excerpt: Three health zones (Rwampara, Mongbwalu, Bunia) in Ituri Province with confirmed BDBV cases. Six health areas affected. First BVD outbreak since 2012.

entries.publisher: World Health Organization

entries.license_note: Africa CDC page terms were not located in this audit pass. Treat the archived raw bytes as restricted publisher material unless Africa CDC reuse terms or permission are confirmed.

normalized_content.declaration_text: PHECS (Public Health Emergency of Continental Security) declared on 18 May 2026
normalized_content.narrative_excerpt: About 395 suspected cases and 106 associated deaths reported. Two confirmed cases in Uganda (Kampala) with one death. Affected DRC health zones: Mongwalu, Rwampara, and Bunia.

spelling_variants.mongbwalu: Africa CDC source uses 'Mongwalu' (no second 'b'). WHO DON 602 and Wikipedia use 'Mongbwalu'. Same place; we adopt WHO/Wikipedia spelling internally and note the Africa CDC variant here.

entries.license_note: Recorded as CC BY-NC-ND 4.0 per the report's stated terms at access time and consistent with repo-wide usage. Treat this as a restricted-use assumption pending primary license-footer confirmation. See evidence chain ec:lovs:archive:imperial-report-license:2026-05-20.

normalized_content.estimate_total_cases_drc: 400 to 800 cases as of 17 May 2026, with values over 1000 not excluded given current data
methodology_summary: Approach 1: population-movement data combined with evidence of two exported cases detected in Uganda
Approach 2: reported suspected deaths combined with case-fatality ratio and time from symptom onset to death from previous Bundibugyo outbreaks
normalized_content.narrative_excerpt: Joint WHO-Imperial estimate. Authors affiliated with WHO Health Emergencies Programme, WHO Uganda, WHO AFRO, and Imperial College London.
normalized_content.report_title: Estimation of the size of the outbreak of Ebola disease caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo

A cross-border corridor-risk view as a structured watch list

entries.publisher: US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention

corridor_signals: four active cross-border exposure clusters identified in Ntoroko District (Uganda) under investigation
mobile laboratories deploying to Mongbwalu and Mahagi (Ituri-Uganda border)
Uganda deploying a mobile laboratory to Kasese near the DRC border
Uganda regional EOCs activated in Fort Portal, Arua, Yumbe, Kampala, Kabale, Hoima
normalized_content.narrative_excerpt: Bunia functions as a commercial and transport hub facilitating substantial cross-border movement; transmission risk amplified by mining, insecurity, weak contact follow-up, delayed detection, and movement of symptomatic individuals across borders.

normalized_content.cfr_grounding: US CDC two prior BDBV outbreaks 55 deaths / 169 cases = 32.5% central; Wilson 95% CI [26%, 40%]
normalized_content.estimate_total_cases_drc: approximately 400 to 900 cases as of 20 May 2026, with values over 1000 not excluded
methodology_summary: Method 1: population-movement extrapolation from the 2 exported Uganda cases plus WHO point-of-entry traveller-screening data shared 16 May 2026 (epiweeks 10/11/15/18)
Method 2: deaths-back-projection via CFR and the Rosello et al. 2015 onset-to-death gamma
normalized_content.report_title: Estimation of the size of the Ebola outbreak caused by Bundibugyo virus in the Democratic Republic of the Congo: May 20, 2026 update

entries.publisher: European Centre for Disease Prevention and Control

normalized_content.capture_role: restricted raw dashboard provenance; superseded for source-review display by drc-moh-epidemie-dashboard-sitrep-008-graphql-2026-05-23 and for corridor source load by the reviewed SitRep 007 PDF cumulative table
normalized_content.interpretation_caveat: Structured table semantics are not yet pinned. Sitrep/008 values appear to be recent/new report values, while SitRep 007 values in the same payload appear cumulative. Do not promote headline counts without resolving daily-delta versus cumulative meaning.
normalized_content.query: epidemie(id: "ebola-bundibugyo-2026", idType: SLUG) { rapportsHebdomandaires(first: 52) { reportsFields { dateRapportage datePublication situationProvince { province { nom zoneSante { nom casConfirmes casSuspects deces } } } } } }

entries.publisher: Ministere de la Sante Publique, Hygiene et Prevoyance sociale, Democratic Republic of the Congo

normalized_content.date_field_caveat: date_rapportage and date_publication are the dashboard API fields. For linked PDFs, verify the printed report/publication dates in the PDF before final manifest ingest; the API and PDF label can differ.
normalized_content.interpretation_caveat: DRC MoH dashboard fields are official, but the GraphQL zone rows must be tied back to the report/PDF table before using them as headline cumulative counts. SitRep 007 exposes cumulative rows; the latest dashboard rows may represent daily/new values until the matching PDF or table label is verified.
normalized_content.query: query EpidemieDashboard { epidemie(id: "ebola-bundibugyo-2026", idType: SLUG) { name epidemiesFields { codeOms dateDebut statut souche } rapportsHebdomandaires(first: 52) { edges { node { slug title reportsFields { dateRapportage datePublication pdfOfficiel { node { mediaItemUrl } } situationProvince { province { nom zoneSante { nom casConfirmes casSuspects deces } } } } } } } } }
normalized_content.row_total_caveat: These are summed health-zone rows from the dashboard payload. They may exclude non-zone rows in the PDF, such as samples without forms, and must not be treated as headline cumulative totals without report-level review.

entries.publisher: Ministere de la Sante Publique, Hygiene et Prevoyance sociale, Democratic Republic of the Congo

normalized_content.date_field_caveat: The dashboard API date fields for this linked PDF lag the printed PDF/report labels. The manifest date policy uses the PDF-visible report date 2026-05-21 and publication date 2026-05-22.
normalized_content.new_confirmed_delta_note: PDF prose/Table I reports 19 new confirmed cases; these are not cumulative source-load counts.
normalized_content.query: query EpidemieDashboard { epidemie(id: "ebola-bundibugyo-2026", idType: SLUG) { name epidemiesFields { codeOms dateDebut statut souche } rapportsHebdomandaires(first: 52) { edges { node { slug title reportsFields { dateRapportage datePublication pdfOfficiel { node { mediaItemUrl } } situationProvince { province { nom zoneSante { nom casConfirmes casSuspects deces } } } } } } } } }
normalized_content.row_total_caveat: Affected-health-zone rows sum to 79 confirmed cases. The cumulative PDF headline is 83 confirmed cases because it includes 4 confirmed samples without case-investigation forms (Echantillons sans fiche), which are retained as unallocated headline context.
normalized_content.table_semantics_evidence: PDF Table I is explicitly a new/delta table (nouveaux cas). PDF Table IV is a separate cumulative table for suspected cases, suspected deaths, confirmed cases, and contacts by health zone as of 21 May 2026.
